objects.name: CVE-2025-20700
objects.description: Missing Authentication for GATT Services in Airoha-based Bluetooth headphones and earbuds. Attackers in proximity can connect via BLE and access exposed services, including the RACE protocol, without prior pairing or authentication.

objects.name: CVE-2025-20701
objects.description: Missing Authentication for Bluetooth BR/EDR (Classic) in Airoha-based Bluetooth headphones and earbuds. Attackers in proximity can connect via Bluetooth Classic and access exposed services, including audio and RACE protocol, without prior pairing or authentication.

objects.name: CVE-2025-20702
objects.description: Critical Capabilities in Custom Protocol (RACE) in Airoha-based Bluetooth headphones and earbuds. The RACE protocol, exposed over BLE and Bluetooth Classic, allows unauthenticated attackers to read and write arbitrary locations in flash and RAM, retrieve device addresses, and potentially extract sensitive data or execute arbitrary code.

objects.name: Unauthenticated Bluetooth Connection
objects.description: Attackers connect to vulnerable Bluetooth headphones or earbuds without prior pairing, leveraging missing authentication in BLE (GATT) or Bluetooth Classic (BR/EDR) to access services and protocols.

objects.name: RACE Protocol Abuse
objects.description: Attackers exploit the RACE protocol exposed on Bluetooth devices to read and write arbitrary memory, extract firmware, configuration, and cryptographic keys, and potentially execute arbitrary code.

objects.name: Bluetooth Peripheral Impersonation
objects.description: Attackers extract Bluetooth Link Keys and device addresses from vulnerable headphones, then impersonate trusted peripherals to connect to victim phones and access privileged Bluetooth profiles and data.

objects.name: Eavesdropping via Hands-Free Profile (HfP)
objects.description: Attackers establish unauthorized audio connections to headphones using the Bluetooth Hands-Free Profile, enabling microphone access and eavesdropping.

objects.name: Remote Control of Phone via HfP
objects.description: Attackers impersonate headphones to connect to victim phones and use HfP commands (e.g., AT+CNUM, AT+BVRA) to extract phone numbers, access contacts, trigger voice assistants, make or accept calls, and perform other actions.

objects.name: Firmware Extraction and Modification
objects.description: Attackers use the RACE protocol to read device firmware and cryptographic keys, and write malicious firmware to the update partition, enabling persistent compromise of Bluetooth headphones.

objects.abstract: List of confirmed vulnerable devices (Airoha-based headphones and earbuds)
objects.content: Beyerdynamic Amiron 300, Bose QuietComfort Earbuds, EarisMax Bluetooth Auracast Sender, Jabra Elite 8 Active, JBL Endurance Race 2, JBL Live Buds 3, Jlab Epic Air Sport ANC, Marshall ACTON III, Marshall MAJOR V, Marshall MINOR IV, Marshall MOTIF II, Marshall STANMORE III, Marshall WOBURN III, MoerLabs EchoBeatz, Sony Link Buds S, Sony ULT Wear, Sony WF-1000XM3, Sony WF-1000XM4, Sony WF-1000XM5, Sony WF-C500, Sony WF-C510-GFP, Sony WH-1000XM4, Sony WH-1000XM5, Sony WH-1000XM6, Sony WH-CH520, Sony WH-CH720N, Sony WH-XB910N, Sony WI-C100, Teufel Tatws2. This list is not exhaustive.

objects.name: Airoha Bluetooth SDK
objects.description: SDK for Bluetooth SoCs used in headphones and earbuds; vulnerable to CVE-2025-20700, CVE-2025-20701, CVE-2025-20702 prior to patched release.

objects.name: RACE Protocol
objects.description: Custom protocol used in Airoha-based Bluetooth devices, exposed over USB HID, Bluetooth Classic (RFCOMM), and BLE (GATT). Allows reading/writing RAM and flash, and retrieving device info.

objects.name: BTstack
objects.description: Bluetooth host stack used for proof-of-concept attacks and HfP exploitation.

objects.name: RACE Toolkit
objects.description: Tool for researchers and users to check whether Bluetooth devices are vulnerable to RACE protocol attacks.

objects.name: airoha-firmware-parser
objects.description: Tool to unpack Airoha firmware update files (FOTA images).

objects.name: Beyerdynamic Amiron 300
objects.description: Bluetooth headphones confirmed vulnerable to Airoha Bluetooth vulnerabilities.

objects.name: Bose QuietComfort Earbuds
objects.description: Bluetooth earbuds; Classic pairing vulnerability present but unreliably exploitable; no GATT exposed.

objects.name: EarisMax Bluetooth Auracast Sender
objects.description: Bluetooth audio sender confirmed vulnerable to Airoha Bluetooth vulnerabilities.

objects.name: Jabra Elite 8 Active
objects.description: Bluetooth earbuds; not vulnerable to Classic pairing flaw due to enforced pairing; no GATT exposed.

objects.name: Ubertooth One
objects.description: Bluetooth monitoring hardware used to discover Bluetooth Classic addresses for attack setup.
Airoha Bluetooth SDK includes the RACE Protocol implementation.
Airoha Bluetooth SDK is vulnerable to CVE-2025-20700 (Missing Authentication for GATT Services).
Airoha Bluetooth SDK is vulnerable to CVE-2025-20701 (Missing Authentication for Bluetooth Classic BR/EDR).
Airoha Bluetooth SDK is vulnerable to CVE-2025-20702 (Critical Capabilities in Custom Protocol RACE).
Unauthenticated Bluetooth Connection attack exploits CVE-2025-20700 (GATT) and CVE-2025-20701 (Classic).
RACE Protocol Abuse attack exploits CVE-2025-20702 (Critical Capabilities in RACE Protocol).
Bluetooth Peripheral Impersonation is enabled by RACE Protocol Abuse, as attackers extract keys via RACE.
Remote Control of Phone via HfP is enabled by Bluetooth Peripheral Impersonation.
Eavesdropping via Hands-Free Profile (HfP) is enabled by Unauthenticated Bluetooth Connection.
Firmware Extraction and Modification is enabled by RACE Protocol Abuse.
RACE Protocol Exploitation Toolkit interacts with RACE Protocol.
RACE Protocol Exploitation Toolkit enables RACE Protocol Abuse.
airoha-firmware-parser analyzes Airoha firmware update files for RACE Protocol reverse engineering.
BTstack is used to implement Bluetooth profiles for attack research, including HfP exploitation.
Ubertooth One is used to discover Bluetooth Classic addresses, facilitating Unauthenticated Bluetooth Connection and attacks.
